learned_facts.fact: Post format optimization: the first 2 lines are visible before the 'see more' fold, so the hook line must grab attention immediately. Use line breaks for readability, aim for 1300-1500 characters for optimal reach, and prefer carousels/document posts which consistently get the highest organic reach.

Engagement timing: comments received in the first hour after posting are critical for the LinkedIn algorithm to boost distribution. Reply to every comment within 2 hours to signal active conversation. Engagement pods (coordinated like/comment groups) are detectable by LinkedIn and result in reduced reach.

Content pillars strategy: maintain 3-5 recurring themes such as industry insights, lessons learned, how-to guides, behind-the-scenes, and contrarian takes. Follow the 80/20 rule where 80% of posts deliver value and only 20% are promotional. Consistency in themes builds audience expectations and trust.

LinkedIn algorithm signals: dwell time (how long readers spend on a post) is a primary ranking factor. Meaningful comments over 5 words carry more weight than reactions. Shares via DM and saves are strong positive signals. Reposts carry less algorithmic weight than original content creation.

Professional networking best practices: always personalize connection requests by referencing shared context (mutual connections, same industry, specific post they wrote). Comment on a person's posts before sending a DM to warm the relationship. Provide value (share a resource, make an introduction, offer insight) before making any ask.
